proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of the conditional average treatment effect when the propensity score is bounded away from {0,1} but the outcome regression admits heavy tails (no uniform sub-Gaussian / sub-exponential moment bound). Find a regime-opening or strict-extension result on the bound width and its dependence on a heavy-tail index alpha.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest CausalSmith catalogue entries (via API.md and the gaps payload's prior-proposal-map): pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec/v1 (bounded f-divergence + bounded sup-norm outcome-regression misspec; does not enter the heavy-tail regime), eid_proximal_phase/v1 (point/interval/Manski phase-transition template, but the obstruction is the proximal-completeness operator singular value sigma_min — a different kind of focal scalar, unrelated to outcome tails), pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity/v1 (covariate-by-covariate odds-ratio sensitivity for QUANTILE-treatment envelopes, not mean endpoints), pid_mte_multi_iv/v1 (primal/dual moment-cone-width for multi-IV PRTE, finite-support LP not heavy-tail). The proposed kernel is the first in-catalogue proposal that (a) characterises the exact moment threshold separating finite from infinite sharp CATE width under MSM(Gamma), and (b) constructs a robust converse witness in weak-* neighborhoods. No prior proposal isolates alpha as the width-controlling parameter under strict overlap.","published_axis":"Published comparators are anchored at the §-level in §4 P2 and §5: KallusMaoZhou2019 (AISTATS, Thm 1 eqn 4) and YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022 (AoS, Thm 3.1) both derive sharp MSM(Gamma) CATE bounds assuming bounded Y a.s.; the gap closed is the moment-existence dichotomy at alpha*=1 when boundedness is dropped, plus the alpha->infty Manski-limit recovery. AsiaeePalBeckHuling2026 (CLeaR, §5) informally notes unbounded-outcome vacuity for LR shifts but does not give an alpha-indexed width law or an iff frontier. JungKang2026 (CLeaR) claims unbounded-outcome conditional bounds via f-divergence duality but their construction is abstract through divergence-feasibility and does not yield alpha-dependent width laws — our sufficiency direction supplies the missing alpha-indexed characterisation that determines when their bounds are non-vacuous. LordanSalehzadehNobari2026 (SSRN) targets confidence-band construction for tail-uncertain ATEs, not alpha-dependent CATE width. No published paper supplies the iff dichotomy at alpha*=1 with a robust weak-* converse witness."}
seed_list: Sharp CATE width under MSM(Gamma) + finite alpha-th moment factorises as W_alpha = kappa(Gamma,eps) * M_alpha(x) in closed form, with critical-alpha*=1 phase transition between finite and infinite width.
Quantile-trimming closed form for MSM(Gamma) sharp CATE upper endpoint extends Kallus-Mao-Zhou by replacing the bounded tail arm with an alpha-Pareto-extremal mean.
Plug-in estimator of MSM(Gamma) sharp CATE under alpha-moment envelope has rate phase transition at alpha=2: parametric n^{-1/2} for alpha>=2, n^{-(alpha-1)/alpha} alpha-stable for alpha in (1,2).
Sharp marginal-sensitivity CATE bounds under covariate-by-covariate odds-ratio + finite alpha-moment yield dual program with alpha-tail-budget cone replacing support box.

Information-theoretic CATE bounds under f-divergence misspecification + alpha-moment outcome envelope strictly tighten Jung-Kang by quantifying the alpha-dependence of the divergence-feasibility radius.
Critical-alpha frontier separating sign-informative from vacuous CATE bounds under MSM(Gamma): alpha > 1 sign-informative iff |E[Y|A=1] - E[Y|A=0]| > kappa*M_alpha.
Selection-probability bounded mean intervals (Aronow-Lee) extended to CATE under finite alpha-moment: closed-form trimming endpoints with sharp width as a function of (pi_min,pi_max,alpha).

Pareto-extremal coupling characterisation of the MSM(Gamma) inner sup: shows the support-only Manski bound is the alpha->infinity limit of the new alpha-tail-budget bound, with strict tightening at any finite alpha.
Necessity-and-sufficiency: under MSM(Gamma) with Gamma>1, finite CATE width on a weak*-open class of outcome laws iff conditional alpha-moment exists for some alpha>1 (iff direction non-trivial).

Heavy-tail-robust orthogonal estimating equation for MSM(Gamma) CATE under finite alpha-moment: Neyman-orthogonal moment with influence function dominated by the alpha-tail-mean of the conditional outcome.

seed_details.one_line: Sharp CATE width under MSM(Gamma) + finite alpha-th moment factorises as W_alpha = kappa(Gamma,eps) * M_alpha(x) in closed form, with critical-alpha*=1 phase transition between finite and infinite width.

anchor_paper.author: Yadlowsky, Namkoong, Basu, Duchi, Tian
anchor_paper.claim: Gives tight CATE and ATE bounds under a Gamma selection-bias condition, plus orthogonal ATE bound estimation, assuming finite second moment and sub-exponential outcome envelope.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: sharp CATE/ATE bounds under a Gamma-bounded odds-ratio condition with sub-exponential outcome envelope (paraphrase of Asmt 3.1)

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not default-to-LP: substrate is naturally an extremal-coupling sup over a non-LP (alpha-moment-ball intersection bounded-odds-ratio set); deploys M11 + M3 + M7 + M9.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Yadlowsky et al. (2022) by replacing the sub-Gaussian moment bound with a finite alpha-moment bound and noting the bound remains finite for alpha > 1.
seed_details.rebuttal: Yadlowsky et al. assume sub-exponential envelopes that imply finite moments of all orders, which is strictly stronger; the closed-form factorisation W_alpha = kappa(Gamma,eps) * M_alpha(x) with an explicit alpha-Pareto-extremal tail-budget M_alpha and the critical-alpha*=1 phase transition between finite and infinite width are not derived in their work, and the alpha < 2 estimation rate is qualitatively new (alpha-stable limit, not Gaussian).

adversarial_referee.instance: Headline names W_alpha as the focal scalar but does §8 actually exhibit M_alpha and the comparator chain reaches a second named comparator?
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Comparator chain is Yadlowsky-2022 (primary, sub-exp envelope) -> Manski-1990 (secondary, support-only) with strict-tightening direction proved at every binding alpha; §9 Exhibit 9.1 computes M_alpha numerically from §6 primitives; §8 names W_alpha = kappa * M_alpha as the focal object.

adversarial_referee.instance: Conjecture 4 promises iff finite-width <=> alpha>1; does §10 sketch the converse direction (alpha=1 implies divergence on an open class)?
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: §10 step 3 of phase-transition chain explicitly addresses the converse via the Pareto-tail witness family and its w*-openness; the necessity direction is the load-bearing one and is given dedicated treatment in Exhibit 9.2.

adversarial_referee.instance: alpha > 1 is sufficient for finite width; if the literature already had a weaker sufficient condition (e.g. finite L^1 norm + uniform tail integrability), the contribution narrows.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 4(iii) gives the necessity direction: finite width on an open class implies alpha > 1, so the condition is genuinely necessary, not merely sufficient; this is the iff tier-bump.

seed_details.one_line: Quantile-trimming closed form for MSM(Gamma) sharp CATE upper endpoint extends Kallus-Mao-Zhou by replacing the bounded tail arm with an alpha-Pareto-extremal mean.

anchor_paper.author: Kallus, Mao, Zhou
anchor_paper.claim: Provides sharp functional interval estimators for CATE under unobserved confounding using adversarial weights with quantile-trimming form.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: sharp CATE interval estimator under MSM(Gamma) with bounded outcomes via quantile-trimming weights (paraphrase from -1.1 gap claim)

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP: avoided — the closed form is non-LP (Pareto-extremal coupling, M11).
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Kallus-Mao-Zhou (2019) since their trimming form is unchanged under any moment envelope.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their form requires bounded outcomes; under alpha-moment R2 the tail arm is the Pareto-extremal mean, which is a different functional with explicit alpha-dependence — this is precisely the gap closed.

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims M11 (non-LP sharp representation) but the form looks like a routine quantile-trimming variant of KMZ19.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The non-LP content sits in T_alpha (alpha-Pareto-extremal mean) which is a closed-form Karamata-extremal integral, not a linear program; the quantile knot lambda* itself is the same form as KMZ but the tail arm is the new object.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only the upper endpoint stated explicitly; lower endpoint and joint identification of (overline-tau, underline-tau) family left implicit.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Symmetric formula stated; jointness of the (overline,underline) pair is by the same primitives; strengthen by stating the joint envelope explicitly in §8 — done in Conjecture 2's wording ('symmetric formula holds').

adversarial_referee.instance: Exhibit on unit Pareto with shape exactly alpha; might be a corner of the feasible class.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Exhibit explicitly saturates the alpha-moment constraint, which is the WORST case in the feasible class, not a corner; this is the extremal coupling the conjecture predicts.

seed_details.one_line: Plug-in estimator of MSM(Gamma) sharp CATE under alpha-moment envelope has rate phase transition at alpha=2: parametric n^{-1/2} for alpha>=2, n^{-(alpha-1)/alpha} alpha-stable for alpha in (1,2).

anchor_paper.author: Dorn, Guo, Kallus
anchor_paper.claim: Derives sharp ATE sensitivity bounds and estimators with double sharpness and double validity at parametric n^{-1/2} rate under finite variance.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: double-sharp double-valid sensitivity inference at root-n rate under finite variance (paraphrase of -1.1 gap claim)

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not single-comparator: chain runs Dorn-Guo-Kallus -> Yadlowsky et al. -> classical Hall (1982) regular-variation tail-mean estimation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from standard alpha-stable Karamata-Hill theory applied to the Pareto-extremal tail-mean inside the Dorn-Guo-Kallus orthogonal moment.
seed_details.rebuttal: The orthogonal moment of DGK is not the same as the plug-in trimming functional here; the new rate is conjectured for the trimming estimator and the achievability of the alpha-stable limit (with the correct scaling constant) requires verifying the joint quantile-and-tail-mean linearisation interacts correctly under MSM — non-trivial.

adversarial_referee.instance: Panel-style estimation-duality floor (M9) typically demands an estimator + rate + orthogonality; orthogonality not yet claimed.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Stated as plug-in (not orthogonal) at v1; orthogonal version is the natural follow-up but the rate result already has flagship content because the rate phase transition itself is new. Honest: this is a strong-field rather than top-flagship result on its own and gains flagship status only as part of the M3+M7+M11+M9 multi-motif bundle.

adversarial_referee.instance: Rate sharpness claim might be only an upper bound; matching lower bound (Le Cam) only sketched.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: §10 names a Le Cam two-point lower bound against alpha-tail perturbations; this is standard in heavy-tail mean estimation and well-established.

adversarial_referee.instance: DGK is at ATE, not CATE; the comparator-claim might shift between papers.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Acknowledged explicitly in §1 P2; the gap closed is both 'CATE vs ATE' and 'alpha < infinity vs finite variance', with the rate phase transition being the load-bearing CATE-level contribution.

seed_details.one_line: Sharp marginal-sensitivity CATE bounds under covariate-by-covariate odds-ratio + finite alpha-moment yield dual program with alpha-tail-budget cone replacing support box.

anchor_paper.author: Yadlowsky et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp CATE/ATE bounds under MSM(Gamma) with covariate-conditional odds-ratio; bounded second moment.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M4-shaped but reframed: substrate is an alpha-moment cone, not a finite-support LP — counts as M11 not M4.
seed_details.referee_objection: Subsumed by seed 1 since seed 1 already characterises the alpha-tail-budget cone.
seed_details.rebuttal: Seed 1 already covers this; this entry is redundant.

seed_details.one_line: Information-theoretic CATE bounds under f-divergence misspecification + alpha-moment outcome envelope strictly tighten Jung-Kang by quantifying the alpha-dependence of the divergence-feasibility radius.

anchor_paper.author: Jung, Kang
anchor_paper.claim: Claims sharp partial-identification of conditional causal effects without bounded outcomes using propensity-derived f-divergence bounds.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: f-divergence propensity bounds without bounded outcomes (paraphrase)

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP avoided: f-divergence ball is convex but non-LP; M11.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Jung-Kang once one substitutes the alpha-moment constraint for the bounded-outcome assumption in their proof.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their proof relies on the divergence-ball structure; the alpha-moment constraint interacts non-trivially with the divergence dual, and the alpha-dependence of the resulting feasibility radius is the open question — but this seed has weaker structural novelty than seed 1.

seed_details.one_line: Critical-alpha frontier separating sign-informative from vacuous CATE bounds under MSM(Gamma).

anchor_paper.author: Asiaee, Pal, Beck, Huling
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp LR outcome-shift bounds flagging unbounded-outcome vacuity.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: unbounded outcomes can produce vacuous worst-case bounds without tail restrictions (paraphrase)

seed_details.referee_objection: Already inside seed 1 as the iff direction of Conjecture 4.

seed_details.one_line: Selection-probability bounded mean intervals (Aronow-Lee) extended to CATE under finite alpha-moment.

anchor_paper.author: Aronow, Lee
anchor_paper.claim: Sharp population-mean bounds under unknown selection probabilities in [pi_min, pi_max].
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: sharp interval estimators of population means under bounded but unknown selection probabilities (paraphrase)

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Selection-probability is the same as MSM via reparametrisation — overlap with seed 1.
seed_details.referee_objection: Selection-probability bounds and MSM are equivalent up to reparametrisation; this seed is seed 1 in different notation.

seed_details.one_line: Pareto-extremal coupling characterisation of MSM inner sup: support-only Manski is the alpha->infty limit of the alpha-tail-budget bound.

anchor_paper.author: Manski
anchor_paper.claim: Worst-case treatment-effect bounds under bounded outcome support.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: sharp worst-case bounds when outcomes are bounded a priori (paraphrase)

seed_details.one_line: Necessity-and-sufficiency: finite CATE width on weak*-open class iff conditional alpha-moment exists for some alpha>1.

anchor_paper.author: Jung, Kang
anchor_paper.claim: Conditional causal bounds without bounded outcomes via f-divergence; no critical alpha.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: f-divergence-based unbounded-outcome bounds without critical tail index (paraphrase)

seed_details.one_line: Heavy-tail-robust orthogonal estimating equation for MSM(Gamma) CATE under finite alpha-moment with Neyman-orthogonal influence function dominated by the alpha-tail-mean.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Double/debiased machine learning with Neyman-orthogonal moments for treatment and structural parameters.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Neyman-orthogonal moments for doubly-robust treatment effect inference (paraphrase)

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Natural M9 extension of seed 1 (estimation-duality with orthogonalisation).
seed_details.referee_objection: Standard application of DML to the alpha-tail-mean functional.
seed_details.rebuttal: Heavy-tail orthogonality requires verifying that the alpha-truncated influence function lies in L^{2 ∧ alpha/(alpha-1)} and that the bias correction does not introduce a non-orthogonal alpha-tail residual — open and non-trivial. Natural follow-up to seed 1.

proposed_from.literature_map: Manski (1990, AER) anchors worst-case treatment-effect bounds under bounded outcome support; bounded support directly controls width. Aronow-Lee (2013, Biometrika) sharp mean intervals under bounded unknown selection probabilities; tension with overlap-bounded weighting under unbounded outcomes. Miratrix-Wager-Zubizarreta (2018, Biometrika) tighten selection bounds via shape restrictions; heavy-tail shape classes are outside. Masten-Poirier (2018, Ectj) parametrise departures from conditional independence via conditional assignment probabilities. Tan (2006, JASA) defines the marginal sensitivity model MSM(Gamma) on the conditional odds-ratio of the full-data propensity. Kallus-Mao-Zhou (2019, AISTATS) provide sharp CATE intervals under MSM with bounded outcomes via quantile-trimming form. Yadlowsky-Namkoong-Basu-Duchi-Tian (2022, AOS) give sharp CATE/ATE bounds under MSM(Gamma) with finite second moment + sub-exponential envelope, plus orthogonal inference — closest comparator. Dorn-Guo-Kallus (2025, JASA) deliver double-valid/double-sharp orthogonal MSM bound inference for ATE under finite variance. Tudball et al. (2023, Biometrika) frame partial-ID endpoints as stochastic-program optimal values. Lee-Weidner (2021, arXiv) span Manski-to-IPW via limited pooling; addresses overlap not heavy tails. Asiaee-Pal-Beck-Huling (2026, CLeaR) derive sharp LR outcome-shift bounds and flag the unbounded-outcome vacuity problem. Lordan-Salehzadeh Nobari (2026, SSRN) frame Manski-style endpoints as support-dependent under heavy tails. Jung-Kang (2026, CLeaR) achieve unbounded-outcome conditional bounds via f-divergence duality but do not isolate a critical tail index. Central tension: overlap controls treatment weighting but the identified-set width is governed by tail allocation; no published source gives a clean alpha-indexed sharp width W_alpha(Gamma,eps,x) with a phase transition.

Comparator promise table emitted below.","SC7_no_tautological_iff":"PASS — Conjecture 1's iff is 'W_Gamma(P)(x) finite <=> alpha*(P;x,a)>1 for some alpha>1'. SC7 passes.","SC8_tuning_parameter_bounded":"PASS — alpha is tied to §7 A3 (conditional alpha-th moment bound M_alpha(x)) and to §7 A5 (regular-variation tail exponent beta(x,a)).
